title: eugenia stipularis
intro: Eugenia stipularis, also known as the strawberry guava or mountain apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and is commonly used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eugenia stipularis include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues, as well as its potential to protect against certain diseases. Eugenia stipularis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be consumed as a fruit or used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures or capsules, although more research is needed to confirm its full range of uses and benefits.
